methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series F → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.3x) to $75M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Locus Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series F), post-money $2B

data.narrative:
Augury is a Series F robotics and manufacturing software company that has raised $75M to date. The comparable universe in Robotics & Manufacturing comprises one public company (277810.KQ) and eight private peers including Locus Robotics, 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, and Bedrock Robotics. However, the peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables—only one same-stage and one adjacent-stage company were identified—precluding a reliable peer-median valuation approach.

Stage-progression methodology is the sole applicable triangulation method. Using total raised ($75M) as a valuation proxy and applying a typical 1.3x step-up multiplier for Series F progression yields an implied valuation of $98M. This represents the median anchor point in the analysis.

The triangulated valuation range spans $83M (low) to $112M (high), centered on $98M. Key uncertainties stem from the constrained peer universe; a more robust median approach would require additional same-stage or directly comparable private company data points. The reliance on a single methodology also limits confidence in precision. Further diligence on Augury's revenue, growth rate, and customer concentration relative to the five identified private peers would materially inform the range.
